2603.18897.title: Act While Thinking: Accelerating LLM Agents via Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution
2603.18897.paper_summary: LLM-powered agents are emerging as a dominant paradigm for autonomous task solving. Unlike standard inference workloads, agents operate in a strictly serial "LLM-tool" loop, where the LLM must wait for external tool execution at every step. This execution model introduces severe latency bottlenecks. To address this problem, we propose PASTE, a Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution method designed to hide tool latency through speculation. PASTE is based on the insight that although agent requests are semantically diverse, they exhibit stable application level control flows (recurring tool-call sequences) and predictable data dependencies (parameter passing between tools). By exploiting these properties, PASTE improves agent serving performance through speculative tool execution. Experimental results against state of the art baselines show that PASTE reduces average task completion time by 48.5% and improves tool execution throughput by 1.8x.
2603.18897.paper_summary_zh: LLM 驅動的代理正在成為自主任務解決的主導範式。<lb/>與標準推理工作負載不同，代理在嚴格的串行 "LLM-tool" 循環中運作，LLM 必須在每一步等待外部工具執行。<lb/>這種執行模型引入了嚴重的延遲瓶頸。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 PASTE，一種模式感知的推測工具執行方法，旨在通過推測來隱藏工具延遲。<lb/>PASTE 基於這樣的見解：儘管代理請求在語義上是多樣的，但它們展現出穩定的應用層控制流（重複的工具調用序列）和可預測的數據依賴性（工具之間的參數傳遞）。<lb/>通過利用這些特性，PASTE 通過推測工具執行來改善代理服務性能。<lb/>與最先進的基準相比，實驗結果顯示 PASTE 將平均任務完成時間減少了 48.5%，並將工具執行吞吐量提高了 1.8 倍。
2603.18897.author: Yifan Sui et.al.
2603.18897.authors: Yifan Sui, Han Zhao, Rui Ma, Zhiyuan He, Hao Wang, Jianxun Li, Yuqing Yang
